meta.name: 2 Esdras
meta.copyright: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

text.Base: The book of the prophet Ezra son of Seraiah, son of Azariah, son of Hilkiah, son of Shallum, son of Zadok, son of Ahitub,
text.KJV: 1:1 The second booke of the Prophet Esdras the sonne of Saraias, the sonne of Azarias, the sonne of Helchias, the sonne of Sadamias, the sonne of Sadoc, the sonne of Achitob,

text.Base: son of Ahijah, son of Phinehas, son of Eli, son of Amariah, son of Azariah, son of Meraioth, son of Arna, son of Uzzi, son of Borith, son of Abishua, son of Phinehas, son of Eleazar,
text.KJV: 1:2 The sonne of Achias, the sonne of Phinees, the sonne of Heli, the sonne of Amarias, the sonne of Aziei, the sonne of Marimoth, the sonne of Arna, the sonne of Ozias, the sonne of Borith, the sonne of Abisei, the sonne of Phinees, the sonne of Eleazar,

text.Base: son of Aaron, of the tribe of Levi, who was a captive in the country of the Medes in the reign of Artaxerxes, king of the Persians.
text.KJV: 1:3 The sonne of Aaron, of the Tribe of Leui, which was captiue in the land of the Medes, in the reigne of Artaxerxes king of the Persians.

text.Base: The word of the Lord came to me, saying,
text.KJV: 1:4 And the word of the Lord came vnto me, saying,

text.Base: “Go, declare to my people their evil deeds, and to their children the iniquities that they have committed against me, so that they may tell their children’s children.”
text.KJV: 1:5 Goe thy way, and shew my people their sinfull deeds, and their children their wickednes which they haue done against me, that they may tell their childrens children,

text.Base: “I gathered you as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But now, what shall I do to you? I will cast you out from my presence.”
text.KJV: 1:30 I gathered you together, as a henne gathereth her chickens vnder her wings: but now, what shall I doe vnto you? I will cast you out from my face.

text.Base: “I call to witness the gratitude of the people that is to come, whose children rejoice with gladness; though they do not see me with bodily eyes, yet with the spirit they believe the things that I have said.”
text.KJV: 1:37 I take to witnesse the grace of the people to come, whose little ones reioyce in gladnesse: and though they haue not seene me with bodily eyes, yet in spirit they beleeue the thing that I say.

text.Base: “Thus says the Lord: I brought this people out of bondage, and I gave them commandments through my servants the prophets; but they would not listen to them, and made my counsels void.”
text.KJV: 2:1 Thus saith the Lord, I brought this people out of bondage, and I gaue them my Commaundements by my seruants the prophets, whom they would not heare, but despised my counsailes.

text.Base: “Thus says the Lord to Ezra: Tell my people that I will give them the kingdom of Jerusalem, which I was going to give to Israel.”
text.KJV: 2:10 Thus saith the Lord vnto Esdras, Tell my people that I will giue them the kingdome of Hierusalem, which I would haue giuen vnto Israel.

text.Base: I, Ezra, received a command from the Lord on Mount Horeb to go to Israel. When I came to them they rejected me and refused the Lord’s commandment.
text.KJV: 2:33 I Esdras receiued a charge of the Lord vpon the mount Oreb, that I should goe vnto Israel; but when I came vnto them, they set me at nought, and despised the commandement of the Lord.

text.Base: I, Ezra, saw on Mount Zion a great multitude that I could not number, and they all were praising the Lord with songs.
text.KJV: 2:42 I Esdras saw vpon the mount Sion a great people, whom I could not number, and they all praised the Lord with songs.

text.Base: In their midst was a young man of great stature, taller than any of the others, and on the head of each of them he placed a crown, but he was more exalted than they.
text.KJV: 2:43 And in the middest of them there was a young man of a high stature, taller then all the rest, and vpon euery one of their heads he set crownes, and was more exalted, which I marueiled at greatly.

text.Base: He answered and said to me, “He is the Son of God, whom they confessed in the world.” So I began to praise those who had stood valiantly for the name of the Lord.
text.KJV: 2:47 So hee answered, and said vnto me, It is the sonne of God, whom they haue confessed in the world. Then began I greatly to commend them, that stood so stiffely for the Name of the Lord.

text.Base: In the thirtieth year after the destruction of the city, I was in Babylon—I, Salathiel, who am also Ezra. I was troubled as I lay on my bed, and my thoughts welled up in my heart,
text.KJV: 3:1 In the thirtieth yeere after the ruine of the citie, I was in Babylon, and lay troubled vpon my bed, and my thoughts came vp ouer my heart.

text.Base: because I saw the desolation of Zion and the wealth of those who lived in Babylon.
text.KJV: 3:2 For I saw the desolation of Sion, and the wealth of them that dwelt at Babylon.

text.Base: “Yet you did not take away their evil heart from them, so that your law might bring forth fruit in them.”
text.KJV: 3:20 And yet tookest thou not away from them a wicked heart, that thy law might bring forth fruite in them.

text.Base: “You may indeed find individual mortals who have kept your commandments, but nations you will not find.”
text.KJV: 3:36 Thou shalt find that Israel by name hath kept thy precepts: but not the heathen.

text.Base: Then the angel that had been sent to me, whose name was Uriel, gave me an answer
text.KJV: 4:1 And the Angel that was sent vnto me, whose name was Uriel, gaue mee an answere,

text.Base: and said, “Your understanding has utterly failed regarding this world, and do you think you can comprehend the way of the Most High?”
text.KJV: 4:2 And said, Thy heart hath gone too farre in this world, and thinkest thou to comprehend the way of the most High?

text.Base: He said to me, “You cannot understand the things with which you have grown up;”
text.KJV: 4:10 He said moreouer vnto me, Thine owne things, and such as are growen vp with thee, canst thou not know.

text.Base: “how then can your mind comprehend the way of the Most High? And how can one who is already worn out by the corrupt world understand incorruption?”
text.KJV: 4:11 How should thy vessel then bee able to comprehend the way of the highest, and the world being now outwardly corrupted, to vnderstand the corruption that is euident in my sight?

text.Base: He answered me and said, “If you are alive, you will see, and if you live long, you will often marvel; because the age is hurrying swiftly to its end.”
text.KJV: 4:26 Then answered he me, and said, The more thou searchest, the more thou shalt marueile, for the world hasteth fast to passe away,

text.Base: “Now concerning the signs: behold, the days are coming when those who inhabit the earth shall be seized with great terror, and the way of truth shall be hidden, and the land shall be barren of faith.”
text.KJV: 5:1 Neuertheles as concerning the tokens, beholde, the dayes shall come that they which dwell vpon earth, shall bee taken in a great number, and the way of trueth shall be hidden, and the land shall be barren of faith.

text.Base: “But if the Most High grants that you live, you shall see it thrown into confusion after the third period; and the sun shall suddenly begin to shine at night, and the moon during the day.”
text.KJV: 5:4 But if the most high graunt thee to liue, thou shalt see after the third trumpet, that the Sunne shall suddenly shine againe in the night, and the Moone thrice in the day.

text.Base: Then I said, “No, my lord.” And he said to me, “Are you greatly distracted in mind over Israel? Do you love him more than his Maker does?”
text.KJV: 5:33 And I said, Speake on, my Lord: then said he vnto me, thou art sore troubled in minde for Israels sake: louest thou that people better then hee that made them?

text.Base: He said to me, “At the beginning of the circle of the earth, before the portals of the world were in place, and before the assembled winds blew,”
text.KJV: 6:1 And he said vnto me, in the beginning when þ earth was made, before the borders of the world stood, or euer the windes blew,

text.Base: “then I planned these things, and they were made through me and not through another; and the end shall come through me and not through another.”
text.KJV: 6:6 Then did I consider these things, and they all were made through mee alone, and through none other: by mee also they shall be ended, & by none other.

text.Base: “And when the seal is placed upon the age that is about to pass away, then I will show these signs:”
text.KJV: 6:20 And when the world that shal begin to vanish away shall bee finished: then will I shew these tokens, the books shalbe opened before the firmament, and they shall see all together.

text.Base: “And after these, Adam, whom you made lord over all the creatures which you had made; and from him we have all come, the people whom you have chosen.”
text.KJV: 6:54 And after these, Adam also whom thou madest lord of all thy creatures, of him come wee all, and the people also whom thou hast chosen.

text.Base: When I had finished speaking these words, the angel who had been sent to me on the former nights was sent to me again.
text.KJV: 7:1 And when I had made an ende of speaking these words, there was sent vnto mee the Angel which had beene sent vnto mee the nights afore.

text.Base: “For I made the world for their sake, and when Adam transgressed my statutes, what had been made was judged.”
text.KJV: 7:11 Because for their sakes I made the world: and when Adam transgressed my Statutes, then was decreed that now is done.

text.Base: “For my son the Messiah shall be revealed with those who are with him, and those who remain shall rejoice four hundred years.”
text.KJV: 7:28 For my sonne Iesus shall be reuealed with those that be with him, and they that remaine shall reioyce within foure hundred yeeres.

text.Base: “After those years my son the Messiah shall die, and all who draw human breath.”
text.KJV: 7:29 After these yeeres shall my sonne Christ die, and all men that haue life.

text.Base: “Then the world shall be turned back to primeval silence for seven days, as it was at the first beginnings, so that no one shall be left.”
text.KJV: 7:30 And the world shall be turned into the old silence seuen dayes, like as in the former iudgements: so that no man shall remaine.

text.Base: “The earth shall give up those who are asleep in it, and the dust those who rest there in silence; and the chambers shall give up the souls which have been committed to them.”
text.KJV: 7:32 And the earth shall restore those that are asleepe in her, and so shall the dust those that dwell in silence, and the secret places shall deliuer those soules that were committed vnto them.

text.Base: He answered me and said, “When the Most High made the world and Adam and all who have come from him, he first prepared the judgment and the things that belong to the judgment.”
text.KJV: 7:70 There should bee very fewe left peraduenture in an innumerable multitude.

text.Base: He answered me and said, “The Most High made this world for the sake of many, but the world to come for the sake of few.”
text.KJV: 8:1 And he answered me, saying, The most High hath made this world for many, but the world to come for fewe.

text.Base: “Many have been created, but few shall be saved.”
text.KJV: 8:3 There be many created, but few shall be saued.

text.Base: I answered and said, “Then drink your fill of understanding, O my soul, and drink wisdom, O my heart!”
text.KJV: 8:4 So answered I and said, Swallow then downe O my soule, vnderstanding, and deuoure wisedome.

text.Base: He answered me and said, “Measure carefully in your mind, and when you see that some of the predicted signs have passed,”
text.KJV: 9:1 Hee answered me then, and sayde, Measure thou the time diligently in it selfe: and when thou seest part of the signes past, which I haue tolde thee before,

text.Base: “then you will know that it is the very time when the Most High is about to visit the world that he has made.”
text.KJV: 9:2 Then shalt thou vnderstand, that it is the very same time, wherein the highest will begin to visite the world which he made.

text.Base: “We who have received the law and sinned will perish, as well as our heart which received it;”
text.KJV: 9:37 Notwithstanding the law perisheth not, but remaineth in his force.

text.Base: “the law, however, does not perish but remains in its glory.”
text.KJV: 9:38 And when I spake these things in my heart, I looked backe with mine eyes, & vpon the right side I saw a woman, and behold, she mourned, & wept with a loud voyce, and was much grieued in heart, and her clothes were rent, and she had ashes vpon her head.

text.Base: “While I was speaking to her, her face suddenly began to shine exceedingly; her countenance flashed like lightning, so that I was too frightened to approach her.”
text.KJV: 10:25 And it came to passe while I was talking with her, behold her face vpon a sudden shined exceedingly, & her countenance glistered, so that I was afraid of her, and mused what it might be.

text.Base: “And I looked, and behold, the woman was no longer visible to me, but there was a city being built, and a place of huge foundations showed itself.”
text.KJV: 10:27 And I looked, and beholde, the woman appeared vnto me no more, but there was a city builded, and a large place shewed it selfe from the foundations: then was I afraid, and cried with a lowd voice, and said,

text.Base: “The woman whom you saw is Zion, which you now behold as a city being built.”
text.KJV: 10:44 This woman whom thou sawest, is Sion: and whereas she said vnto thee (euen she whom thou seest as a city builded.)

text.Base: On the second night I had a dream: and behold, there came up from the sea an eagle that had twelve feathered wings and three heads.
text.KJV: 11:1 Then saw I a dreame, and beholde, there came vp from the Sea an Eagle, which had twelue feathered wings, & three heads.

text.Base: I looked, and behold, the lion roused out of the forest and roared; and I heard how he uttered a human voice to the eagle, and spoke, saying:
text.KJV: 11:37 And I beheld, and loe, as it were a roaring Lyon, chased out of the wood: and I saw that hee sent out a mans voyce vnto the Eagle, and said,

text.Base: “The fourth has come and has conquered all the creatures that have been before; and has kept the world in his control with much oppression.”
text.KJV: 11:40 And the fourth came and ouercame all the beasts that were past, and had power ouer the world with great fearefulnesse, and ouer the whole compasse of the earth with much wicked oppression, and so long time dwelt he vpon the earth with deceit.

text.Base: While the lion was saying these words to the eagle, I looked,
text.KJV: 12:1 And it came to passe whiles the Lyon spake these words vnto the Eagle, I saw:

text.Base: and behold, the head that remained and the four wings disappeared, and the whole body of the eagle was burned, and the earth was exceedingly terrified.
text.KJV: 12:3 And I saw, and behold, they appeared no more, and the whole body of the Eagle was burnt, so that the earth was in great feare: then awaked I out of the trouble and traunce of my minde, and from great feare, and said vnto my spirit,

text.Base: “As for the lion whom you saw rousing up out of the forest and roaring and speaking to the eagle... he is the Messiah whom the Most High has kept until the end of days.”
text.KJV: 12:31 And the Lyon whom thou sawest rising vp out of the wood, and roaring, and speaking to the Eagle, and rebuking her for her vnrighteousnesse, with all the words which thou hast heard,

text.Base: After seven days I dreamed a dream in the night.
text.KJV: 13:1 And it came to passe after seuen dayes, I dreamed a dreame by night.

text.Base: And behold, a wind arose from the sea and stirred up all its waves.
text.KJV: 13:2 And loe, there arose a winde from the sea that it mooued all the waues thereof.

text.Base: I looked, and behold, this wind brought up out of the midst of the sea something like the figure of a man; and I looked, and behold, that man flew with the clouds of heaven.
text.KJV: 13:3 And I beheld, and loe, that man waxed strong with the thousands of heauen: and when he turned his countenance to looke, all the things trembled that were seene vnder him.

text.Base: “This is the interpretation of the vision: As for your seeing a man coming up from the heart of the sea,”
text.KJV: 13:25 This is the meaning of the vision: Whereas thou sawest a man comming vp from the middest of the Sea:

text.Base: “this is he whom the Most High has been keeping for many ages, who will himself deliver his creation; and he will direct those who are left.”
text.KJV: 13:26 The same is hee whom God the highest hath kept a great season, which by his owne selfe shall deliuer his creature: and hee shall order them that are left behinde.

text.Base: On the third day, while I was sitting under an oak, behold, a voice came out of a bush opposite me and said, “Ezra, Ezra.”
text.KJV: 14:1 And it came to passe, vpon the third day I sate vnder an oke, and behold, there came a voyce out of a bush ouer against me, and said, Esdras, Esdras.

text.Base: Then he said to me, “I revealed myself in a bush and spoke to Moses when my people were in bondage in Egypt;”
text.KJV: 14:3 Then said he vnto me, In the bush I did manifestly reueale my selfe vnto Moses, and talked with him, when my people serued in Egypt.

text.Base: “But prepare for yourself many writing tablets, and take with you Sarea, Dabria, Selemia, Ethanus, and Asiel—these five, who are trained to write rapidly;”
text.KJV: 14:24 But looke thou prepare thee many boxe trees, and take with thee Sarea, Dabria, Selemia, Eranus and Asiel, these fiue which are ready to write swiftly.

text.Base: “In forty days ninety-four books were written.”
text.KJV: 14:44 In fourty dayes they wrote two hundred and foure bookes.

text.Base: “And when the forty days were ended, the Most High spoke to me, saying, ‘Make public the twenty-four books that you wrote first, and let the worthy and the unworthy read them;’”
text.KJV: 14:45 And it came to passe when the fourty dayes were fulfilled, that the Highest spake, saying, The first that thou hast written, publish openly, that the worthy and vnworthy may read it.

text.Base: “‘but keep the seventy that were written last, in order to give them to the wise among your people.’”
text.KJV: 14:46 But keepe the seuenty last, that thou mayest deliuer them onely to such as be wise, among the people.

text.Base: The Lord says, “Behold, speak in the ears of my people the words of the prophecy that I will put in your mouth,”
text.KJV: 15:1 Behold, speake thou in the eares of my people the words of prophesie, which I will put in thy mouth, saith the Lord.

text.Base: “and cause them to be written on paper; for they are trustworthy and true.”
text.KJV: 15:2 And cause them to be written in paper: for they are faithfull and true.

text.Base: “I will no longer keep silence regarding their impious deeds which they have ungodly committed, nor will I tolerate their wicked practices.”
text.KJV: 15:8 I will hold my tongue no more as touching their wickednesse, which they prophanely commit, neither wil I suffer them in those things, in which they wickedly exercise themselues: behold, the innocent & righteous blood cryeth vnto me, and the soules of the iust complaine continually.

text.Base: Woe to you, Babylon and Asia! Woe to you, Egypt and Syria!
text.KJV: 16:1 Woe be vnto thee, Babylon and Asia, woe be vnto thee Egypt and Syria.

text.Base: Behold, God is the judge; fear him! Do not sin any more, and forget your iniquities, to never commit them again; so God will lead you forth and deliver you from all tribulation.
text.KJV: 16:67 Behold, God himselfe is the iudge, feare him: leaue off from your sinnes, and forget your iniquities to medle no more with them for euer, so shall God lead you forth, and deliuer you from all trouble.

text.Base: Woe to those who are bound by their sins and overwhelmed by their iniquities, as a field which is choked with undergrowth and its path overwhelmed with thorns, so that no one can pass through! It is shut off and given up to be consumed by fire.
text.KJV: 16:78 It is left vndressed, and is cast into the fire, to bee consumed therewith.
